user_input: Can I use Respondus Lockdown Browser on library computers?
retrieved_contexts: ['FAQ Question: Can I use Respondus Lockdown Browser on library computers?\nAnswer: Yes, the Respondus Lockdown Browser is installed on all public library computers at all of our library locations, as well as on computers managed at ASU computing sites. To access it, you need to follow these instructions : In the Windows Search bar in the bottom left corner of the screen, search for "Lockdown" (note: searching for "Respondus" will not work.) Click on the link for "Lockdown Browser - Lab Edition" A "Choose Server" pop-up window will appear - select "Canvas at ASU" from the drop-down menu. Log in with your ASURITE ID and password when prompted. Your browser should now be locked and you should see your Canvas courses. Navigate to the appropriate course and quiz/test. To exit the Lockdown Browser, click on the red X in the top right hand corner and answer the prompt as appropriate. Please note that all applications, including Lockdown Browser, are managed by the University Technology Office, not ASU Library. If you have questions or are experiencing technical difficulties using the Lockdown Browser, please contact the UTO Experience Center .', 'FAQ Question: How do I clear the cache and cookies in my web browser?\nAnswer: If you\'re experiencing issues accessing full-text library resources, clearing your browser\'s cache, cookies, and browsing history may resolve the problem. Follow these steps: Confirm the basics first: Are you a current ASU student, faculty, or staff member? Are you signed in to the ASU Library website ? If you answered "yes" to both, try the steps below to clear your cache. You may need to try both methods if the issue persists. Keyboard Shortcut for Most Browsers If you\'re using Internet Explorer, Edge, Google Chrome, or Mozilla Firefox, you can quickly clear your cache using a keyboard shortcut: Open your browser. Press Ctrl ( Cmd ) + Shift + Delete simultaneously on your keyboard. This will open a window where you can clear your cache, cookies, and browsing history. Select the appropriate options and proceed to clear the data. For time range, select\xa0" All time " or " Everything ." Close/quit the browser and restart it after clearing the cache and cookies. Additional Help by Browser: Clear cache in Chrome Clear cache in Firefox Clear cache in Safari Consider the following additional browser-related settings: Disable Browser Extensions : Some extensions, especially ad-blockers or privacy tools, can interfere with resource access. Temporarily disable them to see if access improves. Update Your Browse r: Ensure you\'re using the latest version of your browser, as outdated versions may have compatibility issues. Enable Cookies and JavaScript : Many library resources require cookies and JavaScript. Verify that these are enabled in your browser settings. Check Pop-Up Blocker Settings : Some resources open in new windows. Ensure your pop-up blocker is configured to allow pop-ups from library websites. Use a Supported Browser : While most modern browsers are compatible, some resources may work better with specific browsers. If you encounter issues, try accessing the resource using a different browser. Still having trouble? If these steps do not resolve the issue, please contact us for support. Be sure to include screenshots or a video that shows the full URL in the browser.']
response:
Yes, you can use Respondus Lockdown Browser on library computers. The Respondus Lockdown Browser is installed on all public library computers at all ASU library locations, as well as on computers managed at ASU computing sites. To access it, you need to follow these instructions: 

1. Search for "Lockdown" in the Windows Search bar (note: searching for "Respondus" will not work).
2. Click on the link for "Lockdown Browser - Lab Edition".
3. A "Choose Server" pop-up window will appear - select "Canvas at ASU" from the drop-down menu.
4. Log in with your ASURITE ID and password when prompted.
5. Your browser should now be locked and you should see your Canvas courses. Navigate to the appropriate course and quiz/test.
6. To exit the Lockdown Browser, click on the red X in the top right hand corner and answer the prompt as appropriate.

If you have questions or are experiencing technical difficulties using the Lockdown Browser, please contact the UTO Experience Center.
https://askalibrarian.asu.edu/
reference: Yes, the Respondus Lockdown Browser is installed on all public library computers at all of our library locations, as well as on computers managed at ASU computing sites. To access it, you need to follow these instructions : In the Windows Search bar in the bottom left corner of the screen, search for "Lockdown" (note: searching for "Respondus" will not work.) Click on the link for "Lockdown Browser - Lab Edition" A "Choose Server" pop-up window will appear - select "Canvas at ASU" from the drop-down menu. Log in with your ASURITE ID and password when prompted. Your browser should now be locked and you should see your Canvas courses. Navigate to the appropriate course and quiz/test. To exit the Lockdown Browser, click on the red X in the top right hand corner and answer the prompt as appropriate. Please note that all applications, including Lockdown Browser, are managed by the University Technology Office, not ASU Library. If you have questions or are experiencing technical difficulties using the Lockdown Browser, please contact the UTO Experience Center .

user_input: My instructor wants us to use credible sources. How do I know what's good?
retrieved_contexts: ["FAQ Question: My instructor wants us to use credible sources. How do I know what's good?\nAnswer: Many instructors want you to use factual information that appears in reputable scholarly journals or books rather than opinions or unreliable sources found online from an unknown party.\xa0 Most of our library databases let you limit your search to scholarly resources, but even those can include opinionated sections that may not be what your instructor wants. First, make sure you know what types of resources are acceptable for your assignment!\xa0 Sometimes newspaper articles are acceptable, but sometimes they are not! Book reviews in a scholarly journal may be ok, but maybe not! So check with your instructor or check your syllabus for specific resource types that are allowed or not allowed. Need to learn how to evaluate resources? Read the script or view the tutorial about Evaluating Resources!", 'Research Guide: Misinformation\nLink: https://libguides.asu.edu/misinformation']
response:
To determine what's a good credible source, you should first check with your instructor or review your syllabus to see what types of resources are acceptable for your assignment. Many instructors prefer factual information from reputable scholarly journals or books rather than opinions or unreliable sources found online. 

You can also limit your search to scholarly resources in most of our library databases. However, even scholarly resources can include opinionated sections that may not be suitable for your assignment. For example, book reviews in a scholarly journal may be acceptable, but it depends on your instructor's requirements. Newspaper articles may also be acceptable in some cases, but not in others.

If you need to learn how to evaluate resources, you can read the script or view the tutorial about Evaluating Resources. Additionally, you can visit the Research Guide: Misinformation at https://libguides.asu.edu/misinformation for more information. 
https://libguides.asu.edu/misinformation
reference: Many instructors want you to use factual information that appears in reputable scholarly journals or books rather than opinions or unreliable sources found online from an unknown party.  Most of our library databases let you limit your search to scholarly resources, but even those can include opinionated sections that may not be what your instructor wants. First, make sure you know what types of resources are acceptable for your assignment!  Sometimes newspaper articles are acceptable, but sometimes they are not! Book reviews in a scholarly journal may be ok, but maybe not! So check with your instructor or check your syllabus for specific resource types that are allowed or not allowed. Need to learn how to evaluate resources? Read the script or view the tutorial about Evaluating Resources!

user_input: How do I add people outside of ASU to manage my ASU Research Data Repository datasets?
retrieved_contexts: ['FAQ Question: How do I add people outside of ASU to manage my ASU Research Data Repository datasets?\nAnswer: With our current ASU Research Data Repository authentication process, only submitters with asu.edu emails can create an account. Anyone outside the university can view and access published collections (known as Dataverses), but\xa0cannot directly submit/deposit or manage the metadata. If you have members of your project outside of ASU, you can have them submit their datasets and documentation to a secure sharing space like Google Drive or Dropbox and then upload the files on their behalf. If more direct interaction is desired, ASU sponsors can request that collaborators get an “affiliate ID” (ASURITE) to access ASU systems. The ID expires after one year and has to be renewed by the ASU sponsor at that time. To sponsor and request an ASU ID for your collaborators, fill out the ASU Courtesy Affiliate Request - Add or Renew form . Additional workarounds for research teams may include using the Dataverse API , research notebooks like LabArchives , or the OSF as a collaboration platform, and connecting the OSF to Dataverse (which uses an API token). For information on adding co-owners, see our FAQ entry on adding users or co-owners to my datasets .', 'FAQ Question: How do I add users or co-owners to my datasets in the ASU Research Data Repository?\nAnswer: We are using ASU single sign-on as the only method for signing in, and users add their accounts. Library admins cannot add them before they sign in for the first time, and users have read-only access when they are initially added. ASU Library staff provide permissions. Please contact the library and notify us which user you need added, after they have signed into the repository explorer page for the first time. Accounts are created at the time of first sign-in, and after that, the library repository administration team can add them to the account with the appropriate permission levels and even assign them to groups. If you only want\xa0to list someone\xa0as an author, but not grant permissions to manage your datasets, add their name in the metadata author fields and there is no need for them to sign into the repository. In a nutshell: You invite contributors by giving them the repository link, and they sign in to create an account, and then you request that we grant them access.\xa0The library cannot create accounts for them before they sign in. See Roles and Permissions in the Dataverse Platform Users Guide']
response:
To add people outside of ASU to manage your ASU Research Data Repository datasets, you have a few options. Since only submitters with asu.edu emails can create an account, anyone outside the university can view and access published collections (known as Dataverses), but cannot directly submit or manage the metadata. 

You can have them submit their datasets and documentation to a secure sharing space like Google Drive or Dropbox, and then upload the files on their behalf. Alternatively, ASU sponsors can request that collaborators get an “affiliate ID” (ASURITE) to access ASU systems. The ID expires after one year and has to be renewed by the ASU sponsor at that time. To sponsor and request an ASU ID for your collaborators, you can fill out the ASU Courtesy Affiliate Request - Add or Renew form.

Additional workarounds for research teams may include using the Dataverse API, research notebooks like LabArchives, or the OSF as a collaboration platform, and connecting the OSF to Dataverse (which uses an API token). 

For more information on adding co-owners, you can also refer to the FAQ entry on adding users or co-owners to your datasets. 📊💻
https://askalibrarian.asu.edu/
reference: With our current ASU Research Data Repository authentication process, only submitters with asu.edu emails can create an account. Anyone outside the university can view and access published collections (known as Dataverses), but cannot directly submit/deposit or manage the metadata. If you have members of your project outside of ASU, you can have them submit their datasets and documentation to a secure sharing space like Google Drive or Dropbox and then upload the files on their behalf. If more direct interaction is desired, ASU sponsors can request that collaborators get an “affiliate ID” (ASURITE) to access ASU systems. The ID expires after one year and has to be renewed by the ASU sponsor at that time. To sponsor and request an ASU ID for your collaborators, fill out the ASU Courtesy Affiliate Request - Add or Renew form . Additional workarounds for research teams may include using the Dataverse API , research notebooks like LabArchives , or the OSF as a collaboration platform, and connecting the OSF to Dataverse (which uses an API token). For information on adding co-owners, see our FAQ entry on adding users or co-owners to my datasets .

user_input: Are there due dates or fees for interlibrary loan items? Can I renew ILL items?
retrieved_contexts: ['FAQ Question: Are there due dates or fees for interlibrary loan items? Can I renew ILL items?\nAnswer: Yes, interlibrary loan items have due dates and overdue fees.\xa0 These differ depending on the item type and library who is lending the item to you. Due dates For physical items, you can check the due date on the strap attached to the item, or through your ILLiad account .\xa0 The average loan period for most physical items is between 4 and 8 weeks. Electronically delivered items like articles and ebooks do not need to be returned.\xa0 Your access will automatically expire after 60 days. Overdue fees Most ILL items are free to request, receive, and return. For interlibrary loan items that are not returned by the due date, there is a \xa0there is a $5 per day late fee with a maximum overdue fee of $25, and a default $150.00 replacement fee.\xa0The replacement fee may be adjusted depending on the actual cost of the item billed by the library who owns the item.\xa0 We have a high overdue fee as many libraries will block us from requesting any materials when their items are returned late. Home Delivery items are shipped FedEx 2 Day, and include a FedEx Ground return label.\xa0 You are responsible for overdue, damaged, and lost items. Renewals Renewals are usually not available for interlibrary loan items.\xa0To have ILL staff negotiate an extension on a due date with the owning library, please contact us prior to the due date on the item. For more information, contact the ASU Library Interlibrary Loan Department (Monday-Friday, 8am-5pm): (480) 965-3282 Email: ASU-ILL@asu.edu libguides.asu.edu/ill', 'FAQ Question: What items can I request through interlibrary loan? What can’t I request?\nAnswer: You can request these types of items through interlibrary loan: Books Book chapters (up to 3 per book) Journal articles (up to 3 per journal issue) Government documents Theses and dissertations Some media (CDs, DVDs) Some ebooks These items are not available to request: Textbooks Items for personal/non-academic use Entire journal issues Some media (CDs, DVDs) Some ebooks There is no limit to the amount of requests you can submit.\xa0 However, we recommend that you only request materials you can use in a reasonable amount of time to avoid overdue fees.\xa0 Renewals are not usually available. For more information, contact the ASU Library Interlibrary Loan Department (Monday-Friday, 8am-5pm): (480) 965-3282 Email: ASU-ILL@asu.edu libguides.asu.edu/ill']
response:
Yes, interlibrary loan items have due dates and overdue fees. The due dates differ depending on the item type and the library that is lending the item to you. For physical items, you can check the due date on the strap attached to the item or through your ILLiad account. The average loan period for most physical items is between 4 and 8 weeks. 

Electronically delivered items, such as articles and ebooks, do not need to be returned, and your access will automatically expire after 60 days. 

Most ILL items are free to request, receive, and return. However, for interlibrary loan items that are not returned by the due date, there is a $5 per day late fee with a maximum overdue fee of $25, and a default $150.00 replacement fee. The replacement fee may be adjusted depending on the actual cost of the item billed by the library that owns the item.

Renewals are usually not available for interlibrary loan items. To have ILL staff negotiate an extension on a due date with the owning library, please contact them prior to the due date on the item. You can contact the ASU Library Interlibrary Loan Department (Monday-Friday, 8am-5pm) at (480) 965-3282 or email ASU-ILL@asu.edu for more information. 
https://libguides.asu.edu/ill
reference: Yes, interlibrary loan items have due dates and overdue fees.  These differ depending on the item type and library who is lending the item to you. Due dates For physical items, you can check the due date on the strap attached to the item, or through your ILLiad account .  The average loan period for most physical items is between 4 and 8 weeks. Electronically delivered items like articles and ebooks do not need to be returned.  Your access will automatically expire after 60 days. Overdue fees Most ILL items are free to request, receive, and return. For interlibrary loan items that are not returned by the due date, there is a  there is a $5 per day late fee with a maximum overdue fee of $25, and a default $150.00 replacement fee. The replacement fee may be adjusted depending on the actual cost of the item billed by the library who owns the item.  We have a high overdue fee as many libraries will block us from requesting any materials when their items are returned late. Home Delivery items are shipped FedEx 2 Day, and include a FedEx Ground return label.  You are responsible for overdue, damaged, and lost items. Renewals Renewals are usually not available for interlibrary loan items. To have ILL staff negotiate an extension on a due date with the owning library, please contact us prior to the due date on the item. For more information, contact the ASU Library Interlibrary Loan Department (Monday-Friday, 8am-5pm): (480) 965-3282 Email: ASU-ILL@asu.edu libguides.asu.edu/ill

user_input: What do I do if I received the wrong item through Interlibrary Loan?
retrieved_contexts: ["FAQ Question: What do I do if I received the wrong item through Interlibrary Loan?\nAnswer: For articles: Reply to the original email notice telling us what the problem is and we’ll ask the lending library to send the correct article. If you've deleted the email already, send an email to ASU-ILL@asu.edu .\xa0 Be sure to include the TN# (transaction number). For books or other loans: Please return it to Interlibrary Loan as soon as possible and tell us the problem. You can also call or email us, including the TN# and the problem, at (480) 965-3282 or ASU-ILL@asu.edu . The wrong item will be returned to the lending library and the correct one will be reordered. Interlibrary Loan cannot reorder material\xa0until it has been checked in and returned to the owning library.", 'FAQ Question: Are there due dates or fees for interlibrary loan items? Can I renew ILL items?\nAnswer: Yes, interlibrary loan items have due dates and overdue fees.\xa0 These differ depending on the item type and library who is lending the item to you. Due dates For physical items, you can check the due date on the strap attached to the item, or through your ILLiad account .\xa0 The average loan period for most physical items is between 4 and 8 weeks. Electronically delivered items like articles and ebooks do not need to be returned.\xa0 Your access will automatically expire after 60 days. Overdue fees Most ILL items are free to request, receive, and return. For interlibrary loan items that are not returned by the due date, there is a \xa0there is a $5 per day late fee with a maximum overdue fee of $25, and a default $150.00 replacement fee.\xa0The replacement fee may be adjusted depending on the actual cost of the item billed by the library who owns the item.\xa0 We have a high overdue fee as many libraries will block us from requesting any materials when their items are returned late. Home Delivery items are shipped FedEx 2 Day, and include a FedEx Ground return label.\xa0 You are responsible for overdue, damaged, and lost items. Renewals Renewals are usually not available for interlibrary loan items.\xa0To have ILL staff negotiate an extension on a due date with the owning library, please contact us prior to the due date on the item. For more information, contact the ASU Library Interlibrary Loan Department (Monday-Friday, 8am-5pm): (480) 965-3282 Email: ASU-ILL@asu.edu libguides.asu.edu/ill']
response:
If you received the wrong item through Interlibrary Loan, don't worry, it's an easy fix 🙌. For articles, you can simply reply to the original email notice and let us know what the problem is. We'll ask the lending library to send the correct article. If you've already deleted the email, you can send a new email to ASU-ILL@asu.edu, making sure to include the transaction number (TN#).

For books or other loans, please return the incorrect item to Interlibrary Loan as soon as possible and let us know what the problem is. You can also call or email us at (480) 965-3282 or ASU-ILL@asu.edu, including the TN# and the issue. We'll take care of returning the wrong item to the lending library and reordering the correct one.

Remember to include the TN# in your communication so we can quickly identify the issue and resolve it for you. 

For more information, you can visit: libguides.asu.edu/ill
reference: For articles: Reply to the original email notice telling us what the problem is and we’ll ask the lending library to send the correct article. If you've deleted the email already, send an email to ASU-ILL@asu.edu .  Be sure to include the TN# (transaction number). For books or other loans: Please return it to Interlibrary Loan as soon as possible and tell us the problem. You can also call or email us, including the TN# and the problem, at (480) 965-3282 or ASU-ILL@asu.edu . The wrong item will be returned to the lending library and the correct one will be reordered. Interlibrary Loan cannot reorder material until it has been checked in and returned to the owning library.

Conditions and agreements It is important to remember the following conditions when requesting this service: The named individual has your permission to use your library account to transact library business in your name. You are responsible for all library materials checked out using your ASU Library\xa0account.\xa0 This includes proper return to one of the ASU Library and any fines incurred. Proxies must present their own photo ID card and indicate that they are borrowing\xa0materials under Proxy at time of checkout. The status of all proxies is reviewed annually in May.\xa0Proxy status for all Teaching Assistants expires in May.\xa0To renew, submit a new form. You may give someone who is not a designated proxy permission to check out library materials with your ASU ID card. To do so, please have them present their own ID, your ASU ID and a signed, dated, written permission from you. Questions? Please contact the Circulation Department at circ@asu.edu or 480-965-3605 with any questions. More information: https://lib.asu.edu/access/proxy']
response:
To cancel a request for a book from another ASU library, you can follow these steps: 
1. Go to the library home page at https://lib.asu.edu.
2. In the black header, pause on "Accounts" and click on "My Library Account" from the drop-down list.
3. Sign in with your ASURITE ID and password if prompted.
4. Click on "View Holds" and select the items you no longer want.
5. Cancel the items.

Alternatively, you can call the ASU Library at 480-965-3605, and they will cancel your request for you.

For more information, you can visit the ASU Library website at https://lib.asu.edu.

user_input: Where can I find a permanent link to an item I found in One Search?
retrieved_contexts: ['FAQ Question: Where can I find a permanent link to an item I found in One Search?\nAnswer: To find the persistent URL or permalink for a record you found in One Search... In the short display The short display is usually part of your results list as a numbered item (such as the number 3 item in your results list). Along the top right of the short display for the record, look for and click the three dots icon (...). In the resulting list of action options, click on "permalink" to display a URL that you can copy and paste for future use . In the full record display From the short record in the results list, click the title of the item until you find the longer full record for the item.\xa0 If there were multiple formats listed in the short record, you may need to click on the title in the new numbered list of short records, and then click on the title of an item to get to the full record! Look for the "Send to" section of the record to find the permalink. Then click on "permalink" to display a URL that you can copy and paste for future use.', 'FAQ Question: How do I find an item if I already have the full citation - author, title, etc.?\nAnswer: There are several ways to see if we have the article, book, or other item you need. Use our Citation Linker . Remember to select the appropriate option on the form for: Article\xa0(includes DOI search field) Book Journal\xa0(includes DOI search field) Search by DOI with the DOI (Digital Object Identifier) Resolver Search by the citation information in ASU Library\'s One Search * Place quotation marks around the article title so that the words are searched as that particular phrase. Example: " University of transformation: Michael Crow at Arizona State University "']
response:
To find the permanent link, also known as the permalink, for an item you found in One Search, you can follow these steps. 

If you're looking at the short display of the item in your results list, click the three dots icon (...) at the top right corner of the short display. Then, in the list of action options that appears, click on "permalink" to display the URL that you can copy and paste for future use.

Alternatively, if you're looking at the full record display of the item, you can find the permalink in the "Send to" section of the record. Just click on "permalink" to display the URL that you can copy and paste for future use.

I hope that helps you find the permanent link you need 📚. 
https://askalibrarian.asu.edu/
reference: To find the persistent URL or permalink for a record you found in One Search... In the short display The short display is usually part of your results list as a numbered item (such as the number 3 item in your results list). Along the top right of the short display for the record, look for and click the three dots icon (...). In the resulting list of action options, click on "permalink" to display a URL that you can copy and paste for future use . In the full record display From the short record in the results list, click the title of the item until you find the longer full record for the item.  If there were multiple formats listed in the short record, you may need to click on the title in the new numbered list of short records, and then click on the title of an item to get to the full record! Look for the "Send to" section of the record to find the permalink. Then click on "permalink" to display a URL that you can copy and paste for future use.
